opinion 9

我wǒ觉jué得deI think

我 (I)觉 (feel)得 (particle)

  • 我觉得你说的有道理。I think what you said makes sense.
Read more →
我wǒ感gǎn觉juéI feel / I feel like

我 (I)感 (sense)觉 (feel)

  • 我感觉这样做不太合适。I feel like doing it this way is not very suitable.
Read more →
说shuō实shí话huàto be honest

说 (say)实 (true)话 (words)

  • 说实话，我一开始觉得我学不会中文。To be honest, at first I thought I couldn’t learn Chinese.
Read more →
坦tǎn白bái说shuōfrankly speaking

坦 (frank)白 (plain)说 (say)

  • 坦白说，我对这个结果不太满意。Frankly speaking, I’m not very satisfied with this result.
Read more →
要yào我wǒ说shuōif you ask me

要 (if)我 (I)说 (say)

  • 要我说，这个办法更好。If you ask me, this method is better.
Read more →
我wǒ看kànI’d say / in my view

我 (I)看 (see)

  • 我看这件事没那么简单。I’d say this matter isn’t that simple.
Read more →
看kàn个gè人rén吧badepends on the person

看 (depend)个 (measure)人 (person)吧 (modal)

  • 好不好用？看个人吧。Is it easy to use? It depends on the person.
Read more →
客kè观guān地dì来lái讲jiǎngobjectively speaking

客 (object)观 (view)地 (adverbial)来 (come)讲 (speak)

  • 客观地来讲，这个办法更安全。Objectively speaking, this method is safer.
Read more →
看kàn情qíng况kuàngit depends

看 (depend)情 (situation)况 (status)

  • 明天去不去？看情况吧。Going tomorrow? Depends.
Read more →

pattern 3

不bú但dàn…而ér且qiěnot only…but also

不 (not)但 (only)而 (and)且 (also)

  • 这个方法不但简单，而且效果很好。This method is not only simple, but also effective.
Read more →
虽suī然rán…但dàn是shìalthough…but

虽 (although)然 (so)但 (but)是 (be)

  • 虽然很累，但是我觉得值得。Although I’m tired, I think it was worth it.
Read more →
因yīn为wèi…所suǒ以yǐbecause…so

因 (because)为 (for)所 (that)以 (therefore)

  • 因为他每天练习，所以进步很快。Because he practices every day, he improves quickly.
Read more →

personality 2

抱bào负fùaspiration / ambition

抱 (embrace)负 (carry)

  • 他是一个很有抱负的人。He is a very ambitious person.
Read more →
沉chén稳wěncomposed / steady

沉 (calm)稳 (steady)

  • 他做事很沉稳。He handles things in a composed way.
Read more →

pronunciation 2

弹dàn舌shéto roll the tongue / rolled R sound

弹 (flick)舌 (tongue)

  • 我不会弹舌。I can’t roll my tongue.
Read more →
儿ér化huà音yīnerhua / northern -r sound

儿 (erhua)化 (transform)音 (sound)

  • 北京话里有很多儿化音。Beijing speech has a lot of erhua sounds.
Read more →

reaction 18

真zhēn的de吗mareally?

真 (real)的 (particle)吗 (question)

  • 真的吗？太好了！Really? That’s great!
Read more →
不bú会huì吧bano way / seriously?

不 (not)会 (possible)吧 (modal)

  • 不会吧！你真的做到了？No way! You actually did it?
Read more →
原yuán来lái如rú此cǐI see / so that’s how it is

原 (originally)来 (come)如 (so)此 (this)

  • 原来如此，我明白了。So that’s how it is — I understand now.
Read more →
没méi想xiǎng到dàoI didn’t expect that

没 (not)想 (think)到 (reach)

  • 没想到他会来！I didn’t expect him to come!
Read more →
有yǒu意yì思siinteresting

有 (have)意 (meaning)思 (thought)

  • 这个想法很有意思。This idea is interesting.
Read more →
这zhè样yàng啊aoh, I see

这 (this)样 (way)啊 (modal)

  • 这样啊，那我明白了。Oh, I see — I understand now.
Read more →
难nán怪guàino wonder

难 (hardly)怪 (strange)

  • 难怪他昨天那么累。No wonder he was so tired yesterday.
Read more →
可kě惜xī啦lathat’s a pity

可 (can)惜 (pity)啦 (modal)

  • 你不能来？可惜啦！You can’t come? That’s a pity!
Read more →
那nà必bì须xū的de！of course! / absolutely!

那 (that)必 (must)须 (necessary)的 (particle)

  • 你会继续学中文吗？那必须的！Will you keep learning Chinese? Of course!
Read more →
还hái行xíng吧ba，还hái行xíng吧bait’s okay / just so-so

还 (still)行 (okay)吧 (modal)

  • 这家店怎么样？还行吧，还行吧。How is this restaurant? It’s okay, just so-so.
Read more →
真zhēn的de假jiǎ的defor real? / seriously?

真 (real)的 (particle)假 (fake)

  • 真的假的？你中奖了？For real? You won?
Read more →
完wán蛋dàn了leI’m screwed / it’s over

完 (done)蛋 (egg)了 (particle)

  • 完蛋了，我忘带钱包了。I’m screwed, forgot my wallet.

Playful or genuine “I’m doomed”; common spoken filler.

Read more →
厉lì害haiimpressive / awesome

厉 (severe)害 (harm)

  • 你太厉害了！You’re amazing!

Praise; also describes intense/severe things.

Read more →
居jū然ránunexpectedly / actually

居 (actually)然 (so)

  • 他居然没来！He didn’t show — unbelievable!

Emphasizes surprise more emotionally than the cousin 竟然.

Read more →
怎zěn么me了lewhat’s wrong / what happened

怎 (how)么 (suffix)了 (particle)

  • 你脸色不好，怎么了？You look off, what’s wrong?
Read more →
没méi事shìit’s nothing / it’s fine

没 (not)事 (matter)

  • 没事，我自己来。It’s fine, I’ll do it myself.

Also serves as “you’re welcome” reply to thanks.

Read more →
拜bài托tuōplease / come on

拜 (pray)托 (entrust)

  • 拜托你帮个忙。Please help me out.

Can be a real request or a sarcastic “oh come on”.

Read more →
不bú至zhì于yú吧basurely not that bad

不 (not)至 (reach)于 (to)吧 (modal)

  • 哭成这样？不至于吧。Crying this much? Surely not.
Read more →

relationships 1

一yí见jiàn钟zhōng情qínglove at first sight

一 (one)见 (see)钟 (devote)情 (affection)

  • 他们说他们是一见钟情。They say it was love at first sight.
Read more →

religion 2

基jī督dū教jiàoChristianity

基 (foundation)督 (christ)教 (religion)

  • 基督教在很多国家都有信徒。Christianity has believers in many countries.
Read more →
十shí字zì架jiàcross

十 (ten)字 (character)架 (frame)

  • 教堂上有一个十字架。There is a cross on the church.
Read more →

slang 2

铁tiě粉fěnhardcore fan / big fan

铁 (iron)粉 (fan)

  • 我是这个乐队的铁粉。I’m a hardcore fan of this band.
Read more →
你nǐ懂dǒng的deyou know what I mean

你 (you)懂 (understand)的 (particle)

  • 这个事情有点复杂，你懂的。This thing is a bit complicated, you know what I mean.
Read more →

society 1

服fú兵bīng役yìto serve in the military

服 (serve)兵 (soldier)役 (duty)

  • 在有些国家，年轻人必须服兵役。In some countries, young people must serve in the military.
Read more →

summary 2

总zǒng之zhīin short / anyway

总 (overall)之 (particle)

  • 事情很复杂，总之最后解决了。It was complicated, but in short, it was resolved.
Read more →
最zuì后hòufinally

最 (most)后 (after)

  • 最后我想强调的是，方法比努力更重要。Finally, what I want to emphasize is that method matters more than effort.
Read more →

technology 6

下xià载zàito download

下 (down)载 (load)

  • 我已经下载了这个软件。I already downloaded this software.
Read more →
延yán迟chídelay / latency

延 (delay)迟 (late)

  • 今天网络延迟很高。The network latency is high today.
Read more →
加jiā密mìencryption / to encrypt

加 (add)密 (secret)

  • 这个文件需要加密。This file needs to be encrypted.
Read more →
卡kǎ了leit froze / the connection lagged

卡 (stuck)了 (perfective)

  • 你刚才卡了，我没听清楚。You lagged just now, I didn’t hear clearly.
Read more →
提tí示shìhint / prompt / reminder

提 (raise)示 (show)

  • 你能给我一个提示吗？Can you give me a hint?
Read more →
人rén工gōng智zhì能néngartificial intelligence

人 (human)工 (made)智 (intelligence)能 (ability)

  • 人工智能正在改变很多行业。Artificial intelligence is changing many industries.
Read more →

transition 4

对duì了leby the way / oh right

对 (right)了 (particle)

  • 对了，我差点忘了告诉你一件事。Oh right, I almost forgot to tell you something.
Read more →
说shuō到dào这zhè个gespeaking of this

说 (speak)到 (to)这 (this)个 (measure)

  • 说到这个，我想起来一件事。Speaking of this, I remembered something.
Read more →
顺shùn便biàn说shuō一yí下xiàby the way

顺 (along)便 (convenient)说 (say)一 (one)下 (moment)

  • 顺便说一下，明天我不在。By the way, I won’t be here tomorrow.
Read more →
换huàn个gè话huà题tíchange the subject

换 (change)个 (measure)话 (talk)题 (topic)

  • 别说这个了，换个话题吧。Let’s not talk about this; let’s change the subject.
Read more →

useful-phrases 21

幸xìng会huìpleasure to meet you

幸 (lucky)会 (meet)

  • 幸会幸会，很高兴认识你。Pleasure to meet you, nice to make your acquaintance. Slightly formal/polite.
Read more →
过guò奖jiǎng了leyou flatter me

过 (over)奖 (praise)了 (particle)

  • 你中文说得真好！过奖了，我还在学习。Your Chinese is really good! You flatter me, I’m still learning.
Read more →
积jī累lěi知zhī识shíto accumulate knowledge

积 (accumulate)累 (pile)知 (know)识 (knowledge)

  • 学语言需要慢慢积累知识。Learning a language requires slowly accumulating knowledge.
Read more →
有yǒu道dào理lǐ吗madoes that make sense?

有 (have)道 (way)理 (reason)吗 (question)

  • 我这样说有道理吗？Does what I’m saying make sense?
Read more →
土tǔ生shēng土tǔ长zhǎngborn and raised locally

土 (local)生 (born)长 (raised)

  • 他是土生土长的北京人。He was born and raised in Beijing.
Read more →
一yí见jiàn钟zhōng情qínglove at first sight

一 (one)见 (see)钟 (devote)情 (affection)

  • 他们说他们是一见钟情。They say it was love at first sight.
Read more →
女nǚ士shì优yōu先xiānladies first

女 (female)士 (person)优 (priority)先 (first)

  • 女士优先，请你先进去。Ladies first, please go in first.
Read more →
特tè别bié的de风fēng土tǔ人rén情qíngunique local customs and traditions

特 (special)别 (kind)的 (particle)风 (wind)土 (land)人 (people)情 (feeling)

  • 这个地方有特别的风土人情。This place has unique local customs and traditions.
Read more →
不bù知zhī不bù觉jué地dìunconsciously / without realizing it

不 (not)知 (know)觉 (perceive)地 (adverbial)

  • 不知不觉地，我已经学了一个小时。Without realizing it, I had already studied for an hour.
Read more →
一yì模mú一yí样yàngexactly the same / identical

一 (one)模 (mold)样 (appearance)

  • 这两个东西一模一样。These two things are exactly the same.
Read more →
卡kǎ了leit froze / the connection lagged

卡 (stuck)了 (perfective)

  • 你刚才卡了，我没听清楚。You lagged just now, I didn’t hear clearly.
Read more →
火huǒ起qǐ来láito become popular / blow up online

火 (popular)起 (rise)来 (come)

  • 这个梗最近火起来了。This meme has become popular recently.
Read more →
没méi办bàn法fǎnothing you can do / no choice

没 (not)办 (handle)法 (method)

  • 没办法，只能等了。Nothing to do but wait.
Read more →
怎zěn么me办bànwhat should I do / what now

怎 (how)么 (suffix)办 (handle)

  • 钥匙丢了，怎么办？Lost my key, what do I do?
Read more →
受shòu不bù了liǎocan’t stand it

受 (bear)不 (not)了 (able)

  • 这天气我受不了。Can’t stand this weather.

了 here = liǎo (potential complement).

Read more →
习xí惯guàn了legot used to it

习 (habit)惯 (accustomed)了 (perfective)

  • 早起？习惯了。Wake up early? Used to it.
Read more →
算suàn了leforget it / let it go

算 (count)了 (particle)

  • 算了，别想了。Forget it, stop thinking about it.
Read more →
来lái不bù及jícan’t make it in time

来 (come)不 (not)及 (in time)

  • 来不及了，快走！No time, let’s go!
Read more →
来lái得de及jístill time / can make it

来 (come)得 (able)及 (in time)

  • 还来得及，别急。Still time, don’t rush.
Read more →
怎zěn么me了lewhat’s wrong / what happened

怎 (how)么 (suffix)了 (particle)

  • 你脸色不好，怎么了？You look off, what’s wrong?
Read more →
没méi事shìit’s nothing / it’s fine

没 (not)事 (matter)

  • 没事，我自己来。It’s fine, I’ll do it myself.

Also serves as “you’re welcome” reply to thanks.

Read more →

Idioms / Proverbs / Classical 40

chengyu 27

面miàn红hóng耳ěr赤chìred-faced / flushed

面 (face)红 (red)耳 (ear)赤 (flushed)

  • 他们俩为一点小事吵得面红耳赤。The two of them argued over a trivial matter until they were red in the face.
  • 被老师点名回答时，她紧张得面红耳赤。When called on by the teacher, she got flushed with nervousness.
  • 听到那句玩笑话，他面红耳赤地低下了头。Hearing that joke, he lowered his head, blushing.

Often from embarrassment, anger, or argument.

Read more →
苦kǔ尽jìn甘gān来láiafter bitterness comes sweetness

苦 (bitter)尽 (end)甘 (sweet)来 (come)

  • 奋斗了这么多年，终于苦尽甘来了。After struggling for so many years, the good times have finally come.
  • 她相信只要坚持下去，总会苦尽甘来。She believes that if she keeps going, sweetness will follow the bitterness.
  • 创业头几年很辛苦，现在算是苦尽甘来。The first years of the startup were rough; now we're finally reaping the rewards.

Hardship eventually leads to good results.

Read more →
彻chè头tóu彻chè尾wěithrough and through / complete

彻 (through)头 (head)尾 (tail)

  • 这是一个彻头彻尾的谎言。This is an out-and-out lie.
  • 他是个彻头彻尾的乐观主义者。He's an optimist through and through.
  • 这种做法彻头彻尾地违反了规定。This practice completely violates the rules.

Means completely or downright, often before a negative noun/adjective.

Read more →
炉lú火huǒ纯chún青qīngbrought to perfection

炉 (furnace)火 (fire)纯 (pure)青 (blue-green)

  • 他的厨艺已经到了炉火纯青的地步。His cooking skills have reached the level of perfection.
  • 经过多年练习，她的钢琴技艺炉火纯青。After years of practice, her piano technique is masterful.
  • 这位老师傅的手艺真是炉火纯青。This old craftsman's skill is truly perfected.

Describes a skill that has reached a very high level.

Read more →
礼lǐ尚shàng往wǎng来láicourtesy should be reciprocated

礼 (courtesy)尚 (esteem)往 (go)来 (come)

  • 中国人讲究礼尚往来，他送了礼，我也得回。Chinese culture values reciprocity — he gave a gift, so I have to return one.
  • 礼尚往来嘛，下次该我请客了。Courtesy goes both ways — next time it's my treat.
  • 朋友之间礼尚往来很正常。Reciprocating between friends is perfectly normal.

Used for social reciprocity: favors, gifts, respect, politeness.

Read more →
天tiān伦lún之zhī乐lèfamily happiness

天 (heaven)伦 (relations)之 (of)乐 (joy)

  • 退休后，他在家享受天伦之乐。After retiring, he enjoys family life at home.
  • 周末一家人聚在一起，尽享天伦之乐。On weekends the family gathers and enjoys time together.
  • 工作再忙，也别忘了天伦之乐。No matter how busy work gets, don't forget family happiness.

Literary phrase for joy from family relationships.

Read more →
绞jiǎo尽jìn脑nǎo汁zhīto rack one’s brains

绞 (wring)尽 (exhaust)脑 (brain)汁 (juice)

  • 我绞尽脑汁也想不出答案。I racked my brain but still couldn't think of the answer.
  • 为了这个项目，他绞尽脑汁。He's been racking his brains over this project.
  • 她绞尽脑汁想给孩子起个好名字。She racked her brain trying to find a good name for her child.

Literally to squeeze out all the brain juice; used when thinking very hard.

Read more →
截jié然rán不bù同tóngcompletely different

截 (cut)然 (so)不 (not)同 (same)

  • 南方和北方的饮食习惯截然不同。Eating habits in the south and north are completely different.
  • 他们俩的性格截然不同，却是好朋友。Their personalities are totally different, yet they're close friends.
  • 这次的结果和上次截然不同。This result is completely different from last time.

Useful idiom-like phrase; fairly practical.

Read more →
志zhì同tóng道dào合hélike-minded

志 (aspiration)同 (same)道 (path)合 (match)

  • 我们是志同道合的朋友，所以合作很顺利。We're like-minded friends, so working together goes smoothly.
  • 在这里我遇到了不少志同道合的人。Here I've met many people who share my goals.
  • 他在找志同道合的合伙人一起创业。He's looking for a like-minded partner to start a business with.

Used for people who share goals, ideals, or values.

Read more →
深shēn情qíng厚hòu谊yìdeep affection and friendship

深 (deep)情 (feeling)厚 (thick)谊 (friendship)

  • 感谢大家多年来的深情厚谊。Thank you all for your deep friendship over the years.
  • 这份深情厚谊我一辈子都记得。I'll remember this deep affection my whole life.
  • 两国人民有着深情厚谊。The two peoples share deep friendship.

Formal/literary expression for a strong bond.

Read more →
知zhī音yīn难nán觅mìa true soulmate is hard to find

知 (know)音 (tone)难 (hard)觅 (seek)

  • 人到中年，越发觉得知音难觅。In middle age, you feel even more that true friends are hard to find.
  • 他感慨道：知音难觅啊。He sighed: a real soulmate is hard to come by.
  • 知音难觅，遇到一个要好好珍惜。Soulmates are rare — cherish the one you find.

Literary phrase about finding someone who truly understands you.

Read more →
火huǒ上shàng浇jiāo油yóuto add fuel to the fire

火 (fire)上 (on)浇 (pour)油 (oil)

  • 他正在气头上，你这么说简直是火上浇油。He's furious right now — saying that is just adding fuel to the fire.
  • 媒体的报道无疑是火上浇油。The media coverage was undoubtedly throwing gasoline on the fire.
  • 别在他们吵架的时候火上浇油了。Don't make things worse while they're arguing.

Used when making an already bad situation worse.

Read more →
怒nù火huǒ中zhōng烧shāoburning with anger / seething

怒 (anger)火 (fire)中 (within)烧 (burn)

  • 听到这话，他顿时怒火中烧。Hearing this, he was instantly seething with rage.
  • 看到孩子被欺负，她怒火中烧。Seeing her child bullied, she was burning with anger.
  • 他强压着怒火中烧的感觉，没有发作。He suppressed his burning anger and didn't lash out.

Literary-sounding expression for intense anger.

Read more →
熟shú能néng生shēng巧qiǎopractice makes perfect

熟 (familiar)能 (can)生 (produce)巧 (skill)

  • 别担心，熟能生巧，多练几次就好了。Don't worry — practice makes perfect, just keep practicing.
  • 做菜也是熟能生巧的事。Cooking is also something that improves with practice.
  • 他打字快是因为熟能生巧。He types fast because of constant practice.

Very useful proverb/idiom.

Read more →
大dà器qì晚wǎn成chénggreat talent matures late

大 (great)器 (vessel)晚 (late)成 (achieve)

  • 别着急，有些人就是大器晚成。Don't rush — some people simply bloom late.
  • 他四十岁才出名，真是大器晚成。He became famous at forty — a true late bloomer.
  • 妈妈安慰我说，我是大器晚成型的。Mom comforted me, saying I'm the late-blooming type.

Used for people who succeed later after long development.

Read more →
文wén武wǔ双shuāng全quánskilled in both pen and sword

文 (literary)武 (martial)双 (both)全 (complete)

  • 他不但会写文章，还会打篮球，真是文武双全。He writes well and plays basketball — truly well-rounded.
  • 古代的将军很多都是文武双全。Many ancient generals were skilled in both literature and combat.
  • 她在公司里能写能干，文武双全。At the company she can both write and execute — all-around capable.

Classical/literary praise for someone capable in both intellectual and martial areas.

Read more →
杞qǐ人rén忧yōu天tiānto worry unnecessarily

杞 (Qi state)人 (man)忧 (worry)天 (sky)

  • 你别杞人忧天了，没那么严重。Stop worrying about nothing — it's not that serious.
  • 他总是杞人忧天，担心一堆不会发生的事。He's always fretting over things that won't happen.
  • 这种担心纯属杞人忧天。This kind of worry is completely unfounded.

Literally a man of Qi worrying the sky will fall; imaginary/unnecessary anxiety.

Read more →
画huà蛇shé添tiān足zúdraw a snake and add feet / overdo it

画 (draw)蛇 (snake)添 (add)足 (foot)

  • 你这报告已经很完整，再加内容就是画蛇添足了。Your report is already complete — adding more would be overkill.
  • 解释太多反而画蛇添足。Over-explaining only ruins the effect.
  • 这个设计本来挺好，加这个就画蛇添足了。The design was fine — adding this just makes it worse.

From a fable — adding superfluous detail ruins the work. Used when extra effort backfires.

Read more →
守shǒu株zhū待dài兔tùwait by the stump for a rabbit

守 (guard)株 (stump)待 (wait)兔 (rabbit)

  • 你不主动找工作，光守株待兔可不行。You can't just sit and wait for a job — you have to look.
  • 做生意不能守株待兔，得想办法。In business you can't sit and wait — you have to take action.
  • 他天天守株待兔，希望机会从天上掉下来。He waits around every day hoping opportunity will fall from the sky.

Fable: farmer saw one rabbit hit a stump, then quit farming to wait. Means passive reliance on luck.

Read more →
塞sài翁wēng失shī马mǎold frontier-man lost his horse / blessing in disguise

塞 (frontier)翁 (old man)失 (lose)马 (horse)

  • 丢了工作也别难过，塞翁失马焉知非福。Don't be sad about losing the job — it might be a blessing in disguise.
  • 这事看似不好，可塞翁失马，谁知道呢？It seems bad now, but every cloud has a silver lining — who knows?
  • 塞翁失马的故事告诉我们要看长远。The story of the lost horse teaches us to take the long view.

Often paired with 焉知非福 — bad luck may be good in disguise.

Read more →
一yì举jǔ两liǎng得déone stroke, two gains / kill two birds

一 (one)举 (act)两 (two)得 (gain)

  • 骑车上班既省钱又锻炼，真是一举两得。Biking to work saves money and gives exercise — two birds, one stone.
  • 这个方法一举两得，太聪明了。This approach kills two birds with one stone — brilliant.
  • 学中文还能交朋友，可以说是一举两得。Learning Chinese while making friends — you get two benefits at once.

Very practical. Same idea as English "two birds, one stone".

Read more →
入rù乡xiāng随suí俗súwhen in Rome, do as the Romans

入 (enter)乡 (village)随 (follow)俗 (custom)

  • 来中国就入乡随俗，多用筷子吧。In China, do as the locals do — use chopsticks more.
  • 出国旅游要入乡随俗，尊重当地文化。When traveling abroad, follow local customs and respect their culture.
  • 他刚到日本就开始入乡随俗。As soon as he got to Japan he started following local customs.

Literally "enter village, follow customs". Daily-life usable.

Read more →
自zì相xiāng矛máo盾dùnself-contradictory

自 (self)相 (mutually)矛 (spear)盾 (shield)

  • 他的话前后自相矛盾。What he said contradicts itself.
  • 这个政策有点自相矛盾。This policy is somewhat self-contradictory.
  • 你刚才的解释自相矛盾，我没听懂。Your explanation contradicted itself — I didn't follow.

From a tale of a seller hawking an unstoppable spear and an unbreakable shield.

Read more →
望wàng梅méi止zhǐ渴kěquench thirst by gazing at plums / false hope

望 (gaze)梅 (plum)止 (stop)渴 (thirst)

  • 光想着升职是望梅止渴，得实际行动。Just dreaming of a promotion is wishful thinking — you need action.
  • 看美食视频对减肥的人来说是望梅止渴。For dieters, watching food videos is just imagined relief.
  • 他靠回忆从前的成功来望梅止渴。He comforts himself by recalling past successes — a hollow consolation.

Cao Cao tricked thirsty troops by promising plums ahead. Means consoling with imagination.

Read more →
半bàn途tú而ér废fèigive up halfway

半 (half)途 (way)而 (and)废 (abandon)

  • 学了一半就放弃，太可惜了，别半途而废。It's a shame to quit halfway — don't give up midway.
  • 做事最忌讳半途而废。The worst thing in any task is quitting halfway.
  • 他这个人做什么都半途而废。He gives up halfway on everything he does.

Common warning against quitting partway through.

Read more →
水shuǐ滴dī石shí穿chuāndripping water pierces stone / persistence wins

水 (water)滴 (drip)石 (stone)穿 (pierce)

  • 每天背十个单词，水滴石穿，一年就很多了。Ten words a day adds up — persistence pierces stone.
  • 学习就像水滴石穿，贵在坚持。Learning is like water wearing through stone — the key is persistence.
  • 水滴石穿的道理大家都懂，但能做到的不多。Everyone knows persistence pays, but few actually do it.

Classic image for slow, steady effort beating brute force.

Read more →
井jǐng底dǐ之zhī蛙wāfrog at the bottom of a well / narrow worldview

井 (well)底 (bottom)之 (of)蛙 (frog)

  • 不出去看看，永远是井底之蛙。If you never go out and see, you'll always be a frog in a well.
  • 别做井底之蛙，多了解外面的世界。Don't be narrow-minded — learn more about the world out there.
  • 他出国后才意识到自己以前是井底之蛙。After going abroad he realized how narrow his view had been.

Insult-ish: someone whose view of the world is tiny.

Read more →

classical-flavor 6

别bié来lái无wú恙yànghave you been well since we last met?

别 (part)来 (since)无 (no)恙 (illness)

  • 好久不见，别来无恙吧？Long time no see — have you been well?
  • 老同学，别来无恙，最近怎么样？Old classmate, hope you've been well — how have things been?
  • 他发来一条短信：别来无恙。He sent a message: hope you've been doing okay.

Old-fashioned/literary greeting. Good for classical flavor, not normal daily greeting.

Read more →
文wén武wǔ双shuāng全quánskilled in both pen and sword

文 (literary)武 (martial)双 (both)全 (complete)

  • 他不但会写文章，还会打篮球，真是文武双全。He writes well and plays basketball — truly well-rounded.
  • 古代的将军很多都是文武双全。Many ancient generals were skilled in both literature and combat.
  • 她在公司里能写能干，文武双全。At the company she can both write and execute — all-around capable.

Classical/literary praise for someone capable in both intellectual and martial areas.

Read more →
君jūn君jūn臣chén臣chén父fù父fù子zǐ子zirulers should act as rulers, ministers as ministers, fathers as fathers, sons as sons

君 (ruler)臣 (minister)父 (father)子 (son)

  • 孔子讲究君君臣臣父父子子，强调各守本分。Confucius emphasized everyone fulfilling their proper role.
  • 古代社会非常重视君君臣臣父父子子的伦理。Ancient society highly valued the ethics of everyone in their proper role.
  • 这本书讨论了君君臣臣父父子子的现代意义。This book discusses the modern meaning of Confucian role ethics.

Classical Confucian phrase about proper social roles.

Read more →
舍shě得de一yì身shēn剐guǎ，敢gǎn把bǎ皇huáng帝dì拉lā下xià马mǎone who fears not being cut to pieces dares to unhorse the emperor

舍 (give up)得 (willing)一 (one)身 (body)剐 (cut to pieces)敢 (dare)把 (take)皇 (imperial)帝 (emperor)拉 (pull)下 (down)马 (horse)

  • 他做事有种舍得一身剐，敢把皇帝拉下马的劲儿。He has the kind of nerve to risk it all and take down anyone.
  • 舍得一身剐，敢把皇帝拉下马，揭发腐败需要这种勇气。Exposing corruption takes that kind of risk-it-all courage.
  • 记者就该有舍得一身剐，敢把皇帝拉下马的精神。Journalists should have that fearless spirit to challenge the powerful.

Bold proverb about daring to challenge power despite great risk.

Read more →
望wàng梅méi止zhǐ渴kěquench thirst by gazing at plums / false hope

望 (gaze)梅 (plum)止 (stop)渴 (thirst)

  • 光想着升职是望梅止渴，得实际行动。Just dreaming of a promotion is wishful thinking — you need action.
  • 看美食视频对减肥的人来说是望梅止渴。For dieters, watching food videos is just imagined relief.
  • 他靠回忆从前的成功来望梅止渴。He comforts himself by recalling past successes — a hollow consolation.

Cao Cao tricked thirsty troops by promising plums ahead. Means consoling with imagination.

Read more →
滴dī水shuǐ之zhī恩ēn，当dāng涌yǒng泉quán相xiāng报bàorepay a drop of kindness with a gushing spring

滴 (drip)水 (water)之 (of)恩 (kindness)当 (should)涌 (gush)泉 (spring)相 (mutually)报 (repay)

  • 他常说滴水之恩当涌泉相报，从不忘恩。He often says a small kindness deserves great repayment — he never forgets a favor.
  • 我帮过他一次，他却滴水之恩当涌泉相报。I helped him once, and he repaid me many times over.
  • 中国人讲究滴水之恩，当涌泉相报。Chinese culture values repaying small kindness with great generosity.

Classical gratitude proverb. Strong literary tone.

Read more →

culture 2

礼lǐ尚shàng往wǎng来láicourtesy should be reciprocated

礼 (courtesy)尚 (esteem)往 (go)来 (come)

  • 中国人讲究礼尚往来，他送了礼，我也得回。Chinese culture values reciprocity — he gave a gift, so I have to return one.
  • 礼尚往来嘛，下次该我请客了。Courtesy goes both ways — next time it's my treat.
  • 朋友之间礼尚往来很正常。Reciprocating between friends is perfectly normal.

Used for social reciprocity: favors, gifts, respect, politeness.

Read more →
入rù乡xiāng随suí俗súwhen in Rome, do as the Romans

入 (enter)乡 (village)随 (follow)俗 (custom)

  • 来中国就入乡随俗，多用筷子吧。In China, do as the locals do — use chopsticks more.
  • 出国旅游要入乡随俗，尊重当地文化。When traveling abroad, follow local customs and respect their culture.
  • 他刚到日本就开始入乡随俗。As soon as he got to Japan he started following local customs.

Literally "enter village, follow customs". Daily-life usable.

Read more →

culture-history 3

摸mō着zhe石shí头tou过guò河hécross the river by feeling the stones

摸 (feel)着 (-ing)石 (stone)头 (suffix)过 (cross)河 (river)

  • 这是新业务，只能摸着石头过河。This is a new business — we can only feel our way forward.
  • 改革就是摸着石头过河，一步一步来。Reform means feeling for stones — going step by step.
  • 第一次当父母，都是摸着石头过河。First-time parents are all just figuring it out as they go.

Proceed cautiously through trial and error.

Read more →
塞sài翁wēng失shī马mǎold frontier-man lost his horse / blessing in disguise

塞 (frontier)翁 (old man)失 (lose)马 (horse)

  • 丢了工作也别难过，塞翁失马焉知非福。Don't be sad about losing the job — it might be a blessing in disguise.
  • 这事看似不好，可塞翁失马，谁知道呢？It seems bad now, but every cloud has a silver lining — who knows?
  • 塞翁失马的故事告诉我们要看长远。The story of the lost horse teaches us to take the long view.

Often paired with 焉知非福 — bad luck may be good in disguise.

Read more →
知zhī己jǐ知zhī彼bǐ，百bǎi战zhàn不bú殆dàiknow yourself and your enemy — never lose

知 (know)己 (self)彼 (other)百 (hundred)战 (battle)不 (not)殆 (peril)

  • 做生意要知己知彼，百战不殆。In business, know yourself and your competitor and you'll always win.
  • 面试前先了解公司，知己知彼，百战不殆。Research the company before the interview — knowing both sides ensures success.
  • 知己知彼，百战不殆，这是孙子兵法的智慧。Know yourself and your enemy — this is the wisdom of Sun Tzu.

From Sunzi Art of War. Still quoted in modern speech.

Read more →

emotions 2

面miàn红hóng耳ěr赤chìred-faced / flushed

面 (face)红 (red)耳 (ear)赤 (flushed)

  • 他们俩为一点小事吵得面红耳赤。The two of them argued over a trivial matter until they were red in the face.
  • 被老师点名回答时，她紧张得面红耳赤。When called on by the teacher, she got flushed with nervousness.
  • 听到那句玩笑话，他面红耳赤地低下了头。Hearing that joke, he lowered his head, blushing.

Often from embarrassment, anger, or argument.

Read more →
怒nù火huǒ中zhōng烧shāoburning with anger / seething

怒 (anger)火 (fire)中 (within)烧 (burn)

  • 听到这话，他顿时怒火中烧。Hearing this, he was instantly seething with rage.
  • 看到孩子被欺负，她怒火中烧。Seeing her child bullied, she was burning with anger.
  • 他强压着怒火中烧的感觉，没有发作。He suppressed his burning anger and didn't lash out.

Literary-sounding expression for intense anger.

Read more →

life-advice 18

苦kǔ尽jìn甘gān来láiafter bitterness comes sweetness

苦 (bitter)尽 (end)甘 (sweet)来 (come)

  • 奋斗了这么多年，终于苦尽甘来了。After struggling for so many years, the good times have finally come.
  • 她相信只要坚持下去，总会苦尽甘来。She believes that if she keeps going, sweetness will follow the bitterness.
  • 创业头几年很辛苦，现在算是苦尽甘来。The first years of the startup were rough; now we're finally reaping the rewards.

Hardship eventually leads to good results.

Read more →
吃chī一yí堑qiàn，长zhǎng一yí智zhìlearn from a setback

吃 (eat)一 (one)堑 (pit)长 (gain)智 (wisdom)

  • 这次失败了没关系，吃一堑长一智嘛。It's okay to fail this time — every setback teaches you something.
  • 他吃一堑长一智，再也不轻易相信陌生人了。He learned his lesson and won't easily trust strangers anymore.
  • 吃一堑，长一智，下次小心点就好。You live and learn — just be careful next time.

Proverb: one fall into a pit, one gain in wisdom.

Read more →
熟shú能néng生shēng巧qiǎopractice makes perfect

熟 (familiar)能 (can)生 (produce)巧 (skill)

  • 别担心，熟能生巧，多练几次就好了。Don't worry — practice makes perfect, just keep practicing.
  • 做菜也是熟能生巧的事。Cooking is also something that improves with practice.
  • 他打字快是因为熟能生巧。He types fast because of constant practice.

Very useful proverb/idiom.

Read more →
大dà器qì晚wǎn成chénggreat talent matures late

大 (great)器 (vessel)晚 (late)成 (achieve)

  • 别着急，有些人就是大器晚成。Don't rush — some people simply bloom late.
  • 他四十岁才出名，真是大器晚成。He became famous at forty — a true late bloomer.
  • 妈妈安慰我说，我是大器晚成型的。Mom comforted me, saying I'm the late-blooming type.

Used for people who succeed later after long development.

Read more →
逆nì境jìng出chū人rén才cáihardship produces talent

逆 (adverse)境 (situation)出 (produce)人 (person)才 (talent)

  • 古人说逆境出人才，吃苦不是坏事。The ancients said hardship produces talent — suffering isn't a bad thing.
  • 他相信逆境出人才，所以从不抱怨。He believes adversity breeds talent, so he never complains.
  • 逆境出人才，许多名人都经历过艰难岁月。Hardship produces talent — many famous people have been through tough times.

Proverb-like motivational saying.

Read more →
功gōng到dào自zì然rán成chéngconstant effort brings sure success

功 (effort)到 (arrive)自 (self)然 (so)成 (succeed)

  • 别急着看结果，功到自然成。Don't rush the results — success comes with sustained effort.
  • 她相信功到自然成，每天坚持练习。She believes effort pays off and practices every day.
  • 学语言急不来，功到自然成。You can't rush language learning — effort eventually pays off.

Proverb: when the work is done, success follows naturally.

Read more →
不bú怕pà慢màn，就jiù怕pà站zhànbetter slow progress than standing still

不 (not)怕 (fear)慢 (slow)就 (only)站 (stand still)

  • 学习就是不怕慢，就怕站，每天进步一点。In learning, slow is fine — stopping isn't; just improve daily.
  • 减肥不怕慢，就怕站，关键是坚持。Slow weight loss is okay — the key is not stopping.
  • 他常说不怕慢就怕站，所以每天都跑一点。He often says slow is fine but stopping isn't, so he runs a little every day.

Proverb encouraging steady progress.

Read more →
谦qiān虚xū使shǐ人rén进jìn步bù，骄jiāo傲ào使shǐ人rén落luò后hòumodesty helps one progress; pride makes one fall behind

谦 (modest)虚 (humble)使 (cause)人 (person)进 (advance)步 (step)骄 (proud)傲 (arrogant)落 (fall)后 (behind)

  • 老师常说：谦虚使人进步，骄傲使人落后。The teacher often says: modesty leads to progress, pride leads to falling behind.
  • 成绩好也别骄傲，谦虚使人进步，骄傲使人落后。Good grades aren't a reason to be proud — humility brings progress.
  • 他时刻提醒自己谦虚使人进步，骄傲使人落后。He constantly reminds himself that pride leads to backsliding.

Well-known saying associated with self-improvement and humility.

Read more →
有yǒu志zhì者zhě事shì竟jìng成chéngwhere there is a will, there is a way

有 (have)志 (will)者 (one who)事 (matter)竟 (ultimately)成 (succeed)

  • 别放弃，有志者事竟成。Don't give up — where there's a will, there's a way.
  • 他从小就相信有志者事竟成。Since childhood he's believed that determination leads to success.
  • 有志者事竟成，只要努力就有希望。If you have the will, you'll succeed — there's hope as long as you try.

Common motivational proverb.

Read more →
画huà蛇shé添tiān足zúdraw a snake and add feet / overdo it

画 (draw)蛇 (snake)添 (add)足 (foot)

  • 你这报告已经很完整，再加内容就是画蛇添足了。Your report is already complete — adding more would be overkill.
  • 解释太多反而画蛇添足。Over-explaining only ruins the effect.
  • 这个设计本来挺好，加这个就画蛇添足了。The design was fine — adding this just makes it worse.

From a fable — adding superfluous detail ruins the work. Used when extra effort backfires.

Read more →
守shǒu株zhū待dài兔tùwait by the stump for a rabbit

守 (guard)株 (stump)待 (wait)兔 (rabbit)

  • 你不主动找工作，光守株待兔可不行。You can't just sit and wait for a job — you have to look.
  • 做生意不能守株待兔，得想办法。In business you can't sit and wait — you have to take action.
  • 他天天守株待兔，希望机会从天上掉下来。He waits around every day hoping opportunity will fall from the sky.

Fable: farmer saw one rabbit hit a stump, then quit farming to wait. Means passive reliance on luck.

Read more →
塞sài翁wēng失shī马mǎold frontier-man lost his horse / blessing in disguise

塞 (frontier)翁 (old man)失 (lose)马 (horse)

  • 丢了工作也别难过，塞翁失马焉知非福。Don't be sad about losing the job — it might be a blessing in disguise.
  • 这事看似不好，可塞翁失马，谁知道呢？It seems bad now, but every cloud has a silver lining — who knows?
  • 塞翁失马的故事告诉我们要看长远。The story of the lost horse teaches us to take the long view.

Often paired with 焉知非福 — bad luck may be good in disguise.

Read more →
入rù乡xiāng随suí俗súwhen in Rome, do as the Romans

入 (enter)乡 (village)随 (follow)俗 (custom)

  • 来中国就入乡随俗，多用筷子吧。In China, do as the locals do — use chopsticks more.
  • 出国旅游要入乡随俗，尊重当地文化。When traveling abroad, follow local customs and respect their culture.
  • 他刚到日本就开始入乡随俗。As soon as he got to Japan he started following local customs.

Literally "enter village, follow customs". Daily-life usable.

Read more →
半bàn途tú而ér废fèigive up halfway

半 (half)途 (way)而 (and)废 (abandon)

  • 学了一半就放弃，太可惜了，别半途而废。It's a shame to quit halfway — don't give up midway.
  • 做事最忌讳半途而废。The worst thing in any task is quitting halfway.
  • 他这个人做什么都半途而废。He gives up halfway on everything he does.

Common warning against quitting partway through.

Read more →
水shuǐ滴dī石shí穿chuāndripping water pierces stone / persistence wins

水 (water)滴 (drip)石 (stone)穿 (pierce)

  • 每天背十个单词，水滴石穿，一年就很多了。Ten words a day adds up — persistence pierces stone.
  • 学习就像水滴石穿，贵在坚持。Learning is like water wearing through stone — the key is persistence.
  • 水滴石穿的道理大家都懂，但能做到的不多。Everyone knows persistence pays, but few actually do it.

Classic image for slow, steady effort beating brute force.

Read more →
井jǐng底dǐ之zhī蛙wāfrog at the bottom of a well / narrow worldview

井 (well)底 (bottom)之 (of)蛙 (frog)

  • 不出去看看，永远是井底之蛙。If you never go out and see, you'll always be a frog in a well.
  • 别做井底之蛙，多了解外面的世界。Don't be narrow-minded — learn more about the world out there.
  • 他出国后才意识到自己以前是井底之蛙。After going abroad he realized how narrow his view had been.

Insult-ish: someone whose view of the world is tiny.

Read more →
滴dī水shuǐ之zhī恩ēn，当dāng涌yǒng泉quán相xiāng报bàorepay a drop of kindness with a gushing spring

滴 (drip)水 (water)之 (of)恩 (kindness)当 (should)涌 (gush)泉 (spring)相 (mutually)报 (repay)

  • 他常说滴水之恩当涌泉相报，从不忘恩。He often says a small kindness deserves great repayment — he never forgets a favor.
  • 我帮过他一次，他却滴水之恩当涌泉相报。I helped him once, and he repaid me many times over.
  • 中国人讲究滴水之恩，当涌泉相报。Chinese culture values repaying small kindness with great generosity.

Classical gratitude proverb. Strong literary tone.

Read more →
知zhī己jǐ知zhī彼bǐ，百bǎi战zhàn不bú殆dàiknow yourself and your enemy — never lose

知 (know)己 (self)彼 (other)百 (hundred)战 (battle)不 (not)殆 (peril)

  • 做生意要知己知彼，百战不殆。In business, know yourself and your competitor and you'll always win.
  • 面试前先了解公司，知己知彼，百战不殆。Research the company before the interview — knowing both sides ensures success.
  • 知己知彼，百战不殆，这是孙子兵法的智慧。Know yourself and your enemy — this is the wisdom of Sun Tzu.

From Sunzi Art of War. Still quoted in modern speech.

Read more →

literary 6

银yín装zhuāng素sù裹guǒwrapped in white / covered in snow

银 (silver)装 (dress)素 (white)裹 (wrap)

  • 一夜大雪过后，整座城市银装素裹。After a night of heavy snow, the whole city was wrapped in white.
  • 山顶银装素裹，美得像一幅画。The mountaintop was covered in snow, beautiful as a painting.
  • 清晨推开窗，外面已是银装素裹。Opening the window at dawn, outside was already blanketed in snow.

Poetic description of a snowy white landscape.

Read more →
别bié来lái无wú恙yànghave you been well since we last met?

别 (part)来 (since)无 (no)恙 (illness)

  • 好久不见，别来无恙吧？Long time no see — have you been well?
  • 老同学，别来无恙，最近怎么样？Old classmate, hope you've been well — how have things been?
  • 他发来一条短信：别来无恙。He sent a message: hope you've been doing okay.

Old-fashioned/literary greeting. Good for classical flavor, not normal daily greeting.

Read more →
天tiān伦lún之zhī乐lèfamily happiness

天 (heaven)伦 (relations)之 (of)乐 (joy)

  • 退休后，他在家享受天伦之乐。After retiring, he enjoys family life at home.
  • 周末一家人聚在一起，尽享天伦之乐。On weekends the family gathers and enjoys time together.
  • 工作再忙，也别忘了天伦之乐。No matter how busy work gets, don't forget family happiness.

Literary phrase for joy from family relationships.

Read more →
深shēn情qíng厚hòu谊yìdeep affection and friendship

深 (deep)情 (feeling)厚 (thick)谊 (friendship)

  • 感谢大家多年来的深情厚谊。Thank you all for your deep friendship over the years.
  • 这份深情厚谊我一辈子都记得。I'll remember this deep affection my whole life.
  • 两国人民有着深情厚谊。The two peoples share deep friendship.

Formal/literary expression for a strong bond.

Read more →
知zhī音yīn难nán觅mìa true soulmate is hard to find

知 (know)音 (tone)难 (hard)觅 (seek)

  • 人到中年，越发觉得知音难觅。In middle age, you feel even more that true friends are hard to find.
  • 他感慨道：知音难觅啊。He sighed: a real soulmate is hard to come by.
  • 知音难觅，遇到一个要好好珍惜。Soulmates are rare — cherish the one you find.

Literary phrase about finding someone who truly understands you.

Read more →
君jūn君jūn臣chén臣chén父fù父fù子zǐ子zirulers should act as rulers, ministers as ministers, fathers as fathers, sons as sons

君 (ruler)臣 (minister)父 (father)子 (son)

  • 孔子讲究君君臣臣父父子子，强调各守本分。Confucius emphasized everyone fulfilling their proper role.
  • 古代社会非常重视君君臣臣父父子子的伦理。Ancient society highly valued the ethics of everyone in their proper role.
  • 这本书讨论了君君臣臣父父子子的现代意义。This book discusses the modern meaning of Confucian role ethics.

Classical Confucian phrase about proper social roles.

Read more →

nature 1

银yín装zhuāng素sù裹guǒwrapped in white / covered in snow

银 (silver)装 (dress)素 (white)裹 (wrap)

  • 一夜大雪过后，整座城市银装素裹。After a night of heavy snow, the whole city was wrapped in white.
  • 山顶银装素裹，美得像一幅画。The mountaintop was covered in snow, beautiful as a painting.
  • 清晨推开窗，外面已是银装素裹。Opening the window at dawn, outside was already blanketed in snow.

Poetic description of a snowy white landscape.

Read more →

proverb 11

吃chī一yí堑qiàn，长zhǎng一yí智zhìlearn from a setback

吃 (eat)一 (one)堑 (pit)长 (gain)智 (wisdom)

  • 这次失败了没关系，吃一堑长一智嘛。It's okay to fail this time — every setback teaches you something.
  • 他吃一堑长一智，再也不轻易相信陌生人了。He learned his lesson and won't easily trust strangers anymore.
  • 吃一堑，长一智，下次小心点就好。You live and learn — just be careful next time.

Proverb: one fall into a pit, one gain in wisdom.

Read more →
熟shú能néng生shēng巧qiǎopractice makes perfect

熟 (familiar)能 (can)生 (produce)巧 (skill)

  • 别担心，熟能生巧，多练几次就好了。Don't worry — practice makes perfect, just keep practicing.
  • 做菜也是熟能生巧的事。Cooking is also something that improves with practice.
  • 他打字快是因为熟能生巧。He types fast because of constant practice.

Very useful proverb/idiom.

Read more →
逆nì境jìng出chū人rén才cáihardship produces talent

逆 (adverse)境 (situation)出 (produce)人 (person)才 (talent)

  • 古人说逆境出人才，吃苦不是坏事。The ancients said hardship produces talent — suffering isn't a bad thing.
  • 他相信逆境出人才，所以从不抱怨。He believes adversity breeds talent, so he never complains.
  • 逆境出人才，许多名人都经历过艰难岁月。Hardship produces talent — many famous people have been through tough times.

Proverb-like motivational saying.

Read more →
功gōng到dào自zì然rán成chéngconstant effort brings sure success

功 (effort)到 (arrive)自 (self)然 (so)成 (succeed)

  • 别急着看结果，功到自然成。Don't rush the results — success comes with sustained effort.
  • 她相信功到自然成，每天坚持练习。She believes effort pays off and practices every day.
  • 学语言急不来，功到自然成。You can't rush language learning — effort eventually pays off.

Proverb: when the work is done, success follows naturally.

Read more →
不bú怕pà慢màn，就jiù怕pà站zhànbetter slow progress than standing still

不 (not)怕 (fear)慢 (slow)就 (only)站 (stand still)

  • 学习就是不怕慢，就怕站，每天进步一点。In learning, slow is fine — stopping isn't; just improve daily.
  • 减肥不怕慢，就怕站，关键是坚持。Slow weight loss is okay — the key is not stopping.
  • 他常说不怕慢就怕站，所以每天都跑一点。He often says slow is fine but stopping isn't, so he runs a little every day.

Proverb encouraging steady progress.

Read more →
谦qiān虚xū使shǐ人rén进jìn步bù，骄jiāo傲ào使shǐ人rén落luò后hòumodesty helps one progress; pride makes one fall behind

谦 (modest)虚 (humble)使 (cause)人 (person)进 (advance)步 (step)骄 (proud)傲 (arrogant)落 (fall)后 (behind)

  • 老师常说：谦虚使人进步，骄傲使人落后。The teacher often says: modesty leads to progress, pride leads to falling behind.
  • 成绩好也别骄傲，谦虚使人进步，骄傲使人落后。Good grades aren't a reason to be proud — humility brings progress.
  • 他时刻提醒自己谦虚使人进步，骄傲使人落后。He constantly reminds himself that pride leads to backsliding.

Well-known saying associated with self-improvement and humility.

Read more →
有yǒu志zhì者zhě事shì竟jìng成chéngwhere there is a will, there is a way

有 (have)志 (will)者 (one who)事 (matter)竟 (ultimately)成 (succeed)

  • 别放弃，有志者事竟成。Don't give up — where there's a will, there's a way.
  • 他从小就相信有志者事竟成。Since childhood he's believed that determination leads to success.
  • 有志者事竟成，只要努力就有希望。If you have the will, you'll succeed — there's hope as long as you try.

Common motivational proverb.

Read more →
舍shě得de一yì身shēn剐guǎ，敢gǎn把bǎ皇huáng帝dì拉lā下xià马mǎone who fears not being cut to pieces dares to unhorse the emperor

舍 (give up)得 (willing)一 (one)身 (body)剐 (cut to pieces)敢 (dare)把 (take)皇 (imperial)帝 (emperor)拉 (pull)下 (down)马 (horse)

  • 他做事有种舍得一身剐，敢把皇帝拉下马的劲儿。He has the kind of nerve to risk it all and take down anyone.
  • 舍得一身剐，敢把皇帝拉下马，揭发腐败需要这种勇气。Exposing corruption takes that kind of risk-it-all courage.
  • 记者就该有舍得一身剐，敢把皇帝拉下马的精神。Journalists should have that fearless spirit to challenge the powerful.

Bold proverb about daring to challenge power despite great risk.

Read more →
摸mō着zhe石shí头tou过guò河hécross the river by feeling the stones

摸 (feel)着 (-ing)石 (stone)头 (suffix)过 (cross)河 (river)

  • 这是新业务，只能摸着石头过河。This is a new business — we can only feel our way forward.
  • 改革就是摸着石头过河，一步一步来。Reform means feeling for stones — going step by step.
  • 第一次当父母，都是摸着石头过河。First-time parents are all just figuring it out as they go.

Proceed cautiously through trial and error.

Read more →
滴dī水shuǐ之zhī恩ēn，当dāng涌yǒng泉quán相xiāng报bàorepay a drop of kindness with a gushing spring

滴 (drip)水 (water)之 (of)恩 (kindness)当 (should)涌 (gush)泉 (spring)相 (mutually)报 (repay)

  • 他常说滴水之恩当涌泉相报，从不忘恩。He often says a small kindness deserves great repayment — he never forgets a favor.
  • 我帮过他一次，他却滴水之恩当涌泉相报。I helped him once, and he repaid me many times over.
  • 中国人讲究滴水之恩，当涌泉相报。Chinese culture values repaying small kindness with great generosity.

Classical gratitude proverb. Strong literary tone.

Read more →
知zhī己jǐ知zhī彼bǐ，百bǎi战zhàn不bú殆dàiknow yourself and your enemy — never lose

知 (know)己 (self)彼 (other)百 (hundred)战 (battle)不 (not)殆 (peril)

  • 做生意要知己知彼，百战不殆。In business, know yourself and your competitor and you'll always win.
  • 面试前先了解公司，知己知彼，百战不殆。Research the company before the interview — knowing both sides ensures success.
  • 知己知彼，百战不殆，这是孙子兵法的智慧。Know yourself and your enemy — this is the wisdom of Sun Tzu.

From Sunzi Art of War. Still quoted in modern speech.

Read more →

relationships 3

志zhì同tóng道dào合hélike-minded

志 (aspiration)同 (same)道 (path)合 (match)

  • 我们是志同道合的朋友，所以合作很顺利。We're like-minded friends, so working together goes smoothly.
  • 在这里我遇到了不少志同道合的人。Here I've met many people who share my goals.
  • 他在找志同道合的合伙人一起创业。He's looking for a like-minded partner to start a business with.

Used for people who share goals, ideals, or values.

Read more →
深shēn情qíng厚hòu谊yìdeep affection and friendship

深 (deep)情 (feeling)厚 (thick)谊 (friendship)

  • 感谢大家多年来的深情厚谊。Thank you all for your deep friendship over the years.
  • 这份深情厚谊我一辈子都记得。I'll remember this deep affection my whole life.
  • 两国人民有着深情厚谊。The two peoples share deep friendship.

Formal/literary expression for a strong bond.

Read more →
知zhī音yīn难nán觅mìa true soulmate is hard to find

知 (know)音 (tone)难 (hard)觅 (seek)

  • 人到中年，越发觉得知音难觅。In middle age, you feel even more that true friends are hard to find.
  • 他感慨道：知音难觅啊。He sighed: a real soulmate is hard to come by.
  • 知音难觅，遇到一个要好好珍惜。Soulmates are rare — cherish the one you find.

Literary phrase about finding someone who truly understands you.

Read more →

skill 1

炉lú火huǒ纯chún青qīngbrought to perfection

炉 (furnace)火 (fire)纯 (pure)青 (blue-green)

  • 他的厨艺已经到了炉火纯青的地步。His cooking skills have reached the level of perfection.
  • 经过多年练习，她的钢琴技艺炉火纯青。After years of practice, her piano technique is masterful.
  • 这位老师傅的手艺真是炉火纯青。This old craftsman's skill is truly perfected.

Describes a skill that has reached a very high level.

Read more →

Slang / Dialect / Flavor 20

conversation-glue 1

还hái凑còu合hé吧bait’ll do / passable

还 (still)凑 (gather)合 (fit)吧 (sentence-final)

  • 这饭还凑合吧。This food's just okay.
  • 工作还凑合吧，不算累。The job's passable, not too tiring.
  • 今天天气还凑合吧。The weather today is so-so.

Casual, slightly modest or lukewarm evaluation.

Read more →

culture 2

泰tài斗dǒuleading authority / grand master

泰 (mount tai)斗 (big dipper)

  • 他是中医界的泰斗。He's a leading authority in Chinese medicine.
  • 这位老先生是物理学泰斗。This old gentleman is a grandmaster of physics.
  • 她可是书法界的泰斗。She's a giant in the calligraphy world.

Respectful term for an eminent expert; not exactly casual “GOAT”.

Read more →
辣là妹mèi子zispicy girl / bold girl

辣 (spicy)妹 (girl)子 (suffix)

  • 她是个湖南辣妹子。She's a feisty Hunan girl.
  • 别惹这辣妹子。Don't mess with this spicy girl.
  • 我喜欢辣妹子那种性格。I like that spicy-girl personality.

Colloquial and culturally flavored. Context-sensitive.

Read more →

dialect 5

你nǐ爪zhuǎ子zi嘛mawhat’s up? / what are you doing?

你 (you)爪 (doing[dialect])子 (suffix)嘛 (sentence-final)

  • 你爪子嘛，这么晚还不睡？What are you doing, still up this late?
  • 喂，你爪子嘛呢？Hey, what're you up to?
  • 你爪子嘛，半天不回消息。What're you doing? Haven't replied for ages.

Sichuan dialect flavor. Do not treat as standard Mandarin.

Read more →
杠gàng杠gàng的deawesome / excellent

杠 (bar)的 (particle)

  • 这家店的服务杠杠的。This shop's service is top-notch.
  • 他的技术杠杠的。His skills are awesome.
  • 质量杠杠的，放心买。Quality's solid, buy with confidence.

Northeastern-flavored slang.

Read more →
抗kàng造zàotough / durable / resilient

抗 (resist)造 (wear)

  • 这双鞋真抗造。These shoes are really durable.
  • 他身体特别抗造。He's got a really tough constitution.
  • 这手机挺抗造的，摔了好几次。This phone's tough, dropped it many times.

Northern/casual flavor. Often used for people or things that can take punishment.

Read more →
儿ér豁huōno cap / for real

儿 (son)豁 (deceive[dialect])

  • 我说的是真的，儿豁！I'm telling the truth, no cap!
  • 儿豁，我亲眼看见的。For real, I saw it with my own eyes.
  • 他真的来了，儿豁！He really came, no joke!

Sichuan/Chongqing dialect slang. Used to mean “seriously,” “for real,” or “I’m not lying.” Recognition first unless you know the context.

Read more →
瞅chǒu啥shá呀yawhat the hell are you looking at?

瞅 (look)啥 (what)呀 (sentence-final)

  • 瞅啥呀，没见过啊？What you looking at, never seen one?
  • 你瞅啥呀，有事吗？What you staring at, got a problem?
  • 瞅啥呀，再瞅揍你。What you looking at? Keep it up and I'll hit you.

Northern/rude confrontational phrase. Very tone-sensitive; mostly recognition first.

Read more →

education 1

学xué霸bàstraight-A student / top student

学 (study)霸 (boss)

  • 他是我们班的学霸。He's the top student in our class.
  • 别问我，问学霸去。Don't ask me, go ask the brainiac.
  • 学霸又考了第一名。The straight-A student got first place again.

Common casual word for someone very good at studying.

Read more →

erhua 1

媳xí妇fù儿erwife / wifey

媳 (wife)妇 (woman)儿 (erhua)

  • 我媳妇儿做饭特别好吃。My wifey cooks really well.
  • 今晚我得陪媳妇儿。I gotta hang out with the wife tonight.
  • 他媳妇儿可厉害了。His wife is seriously impressive.

Northern/erhua-flavored casual word. Usage depends on relationship and region.

Read more →

everyday-vocab 1

家jiā伙huoguy / fellow / bloke

家 (home)伙 (fellow)

  • 这家伙真有意思。This guy is really funny.
  • 那家伙又迟到了。That dude is late again.
  • 你这家伙，跑哪儿去了？You, where'd you run off to?

Casual word for a person; tone depends on context.

Read more →

flavor 2

泰tài斗dǒuleading authority / grand master

泰 (mount tai)斗 (big dipper)

  • 他是中医界的泰斗。He's a leading authority in Chinese medicine.
  • 这位老先生是物理学泰斗。This old gentleman is a grandmaster of physics.
  • 她可是书法界的泰斗。She's a giant in the calligraphy world.

Respectful term for an eminent expert; not exactly casual “GOAT”.

Read more →
洒sǎ脱tuōcarefree and unrestrained

洒 (free)脱 (unrestrained)

  • 他这个人很洒脱。He's a really carefree guy.
  • 活得洒脱一点，别想太多。Live a bit more freely, don't overthink.
  • 她说分手就分手，真洒脱。She broke up just like that, so unbothered.

Positive personality descriptor with a stylish/literary feel.

Read more →

food 1

无wú辣là不bù欢huānnot spicy, not happy / can’t enjoy food without spice

无 (no)辣 (spicy)不 (not)欢 (happy)

  • 我是无辣不欢的人。I can't enjoy food without spice.
  • 四川人基本都无辣不欢。Sichuan people basically need spice in everything.
  • 她无辣不欢，顿顿都要辣椒。She needs spice every meal.

Fixed colloquial expression. Good for describing someone who loves spicy food.

Read more →

internet-slang 2

菜cài鸟niǎonoob / newbie

菜 (rookie)鸟 (bird)

  • 我是新来的，还是个菜鸟。I'm new here, still a noob.
  • 别笑我，我打游戏是菜鸟。Don't laugh, I'm a noob at games.
  • 这个菜鸟连基础都不会。This newbie doesn't even know the basics.

Casual internet-style term. Can be teasing.

Read more →
儿ér豁huōno cap / for real

儿 (son)豁 (deceive[dialect])

  • 我说的是真的，儿豁！I'm telling the truth, no cap!
  • 儿豁，我亲眼看见的。For real, I saw it with my own eyes.
  • 他真的来了，儿豁！He really came, no joke!

Sichuan/Chongqing dialect slang. Used to mean “seriously,” “for real,” or “I’m not lying.” Recognition first unless you know the context.

Read more →

old-slang 1

酷kù毙bì了lesuper cool

酷 (cool)毙 (extreme)了 (particle)

  • 这款游戏酷毙了！This game is super cool!
  • 他今天的造型酷毙了。His look today is super cool.
  • 你的新车酷毙了。Your new car is so cool.

Old/dedicated slang. Fun to recognize, but not the most modern everyday expression.

Read more →

personality 1

洒sǎ脱tuōcarefree and unrestrained

洒 (free)脱 (unrestrained)

  • 他这个人很洒脱。He's a really carefree guy.
  • 活得洒脱一点，别想太多。Live a bit more freely, don't overthink.
  • 她说分手就分手，真洒脱。She broke up just like that, so unbothered.

Positive personality descriptor with a stylish/literary feel.

Read more →

reaction 1

哇wā塞sāiwow!

哇 (wow)塞 (interjection)

  • 哇塞，这也太漂亮了吧！Wow, this is so pretty!
  • 哇塞，你怎么瘦这么多？Whoa, how'd you lose so much weight?
  • 哇塞，真的假的？Wow, really?

Casual exclamation. Slightly playful.

Read more →

relationships 1

媳xí妇fù儿erwife / wifey

媳 (wife)妇 (woman)儿 (erhua)

  • 我媳妇儿做饭特别好吃。My wifey cooks really well.
  • 今晚我得陪媳妇儿。I gotta hang out with the wife tonight.
  • 他媳妇儿可厉害了。His wife is seriously impressive.

Northern/erhua-flavored casual word. Usage depends on relationship and region.

Read more →

rude-playful 4

变biàn态tàipervert / abnormal

变 (change)态 (state)

  • 你这个变态，别碰我！You pervert, don't touch me!
  • 这题难得变态。This problem is insanely hard.
  • 他笑得有点变态。His smile is kind of creepy.

Can be insulting or serious depending on context. Use carefully.

Read more →
你nǐ皮pí子zi是shì不bú是shì痒yǎng了le?are you asking for a beating?

你 (you)皮 (skin)子 (suffix)是 (be)不 (not)痒 (itch)了 (particle)

  • 你皮子是不是痒了？敢顶嘴！You asking for a beating? Talking back?!
  • 再闹，你皮子是不是痒了？Keep it up, you want a smack?
  • 你皮子是不是痒了？我揍你啊。You itching for it? I'll hit you.

Rude/playful threat-like phrase. Recognition first; be careful using it.

Read more →
小xiǎo屁pì孩háilittle brat

小 (little)屁 (butt)孩 (kid)

  • 你个小屁孩懂什么？What do you know, you little brat?
  • 一群小屁孩在楼下踢球。A bunch of brats are playing ball downstairs.
  • 别跟小屁孩一般见识。Don't stoop to a kid's level.

Casual insult for a child or immature person. Use carefully.

Read more →
瞅chǒu啥shá呀yawhat the hell are you looking at?

瞅 (look)啥 (what)呀 (sentence-final)

  • 瞅啥呀，没见过啊？What you looking at, never seen one?
  • 你瞅啥呀，有事吗？What you staring at, got a problem?
  • 瞅啥呀，再瞅揍你。What you looking at? Keep it up and I'll hit you.

Northern/rude confrontational phrase. Very tone-sensitive; mostly recognition first.

Read more →

slang 15

酷kù毙bì了lesuper cool

酷 (cool)毙 (extreme)了 (particle)

  • 这款游戏酷毙了！This game is super cool!
  • 他今天的造型酷毙了。His look today is super cool.
  • 你的新车酷毙了。Your new car is so cool.

Old/dedicated slang. Fun to recognize, but not the most modern everyday expression.

Read more →
你nǐ爪zhuǎ子zi嘛mawhat’s up? / what are you doing?

你 (you)爪 (doing[dialect])子 (suffix)嘛 (sentence-final)

  • 你爪子嘛，这么晚还不睡？What are you doing, still up this late?
  • 喂，你爪子嘛呢？Hey, what're you up to?
  • 你爪子嘛，半天不回消息。What're you doing? Haven't replied for ages.

Sichuan dialect flavor. Do not treat as standard Mandarin.

Read more →
学xué霸bàstraight-A student / top student

学 (study)霸 (boss)

  • 他是我们班的学霸。He's the top student in our class.
  • 别问我，问学霸去。Don't ask me, go ask the brainiac.
  • 学霸又考了第一名。The straight-A student got first place again.

Common casual word for someone very good at studying.

Read more →
哇wā塞sāiwow!

哇 (wow)塞 (interjection)

  • 哇塞，这也太漂亮了吧！Wow, this is so pretty!
  • 哇塞，你怎么瘦这么多？Whoa, how'd you lose so much weight?
  • 哇塞，真的假的？Wow, really?

Casual exclamation. Slightly playful.

Read more →
媳xí妇fù儿erwife / wifey

媳 (wife)妇 (woman)儿 (erhua)

  • 我媳妇儿做饭特别好吃。My wifey cooks really well.
  • 今晚我得陪媳妇儿。I gotta hang out with the wife tonight.
  • 他媳妇儿可厉害了。His wife is seriously impressive.

Northern/erhua-flavored casual word. Usage depends on relationship and region.

Read more →
杠gàng杠gàng的deawesome / excellent

杠 (bar)的 (particle)

  • 这家店的服务杠杠的。This shop's service is top-notch.
  • 他的技术杠杠的。His skills are awesome.
  • 质量杠杠的，放心买。Quality's solid, buy with confidence.

Northeastern-flavored slang.

Read more →
还hái凑còu合hé吧bait’ll do / passable

还 (still)凑 (gather)合 (fit)吧 (sentence-final)

  • 这饭还凑合吧。This food's just okay.
  • 工作还凑合吧，不算累。The job's passable, not too tiring.
  • 今天天气还凑合吧。The weather today is so-so.

Casual, slightly modest or lukewarm evaluation.

Read more →
菜cài鸟niǎonoob / newbie

菜 (rookie)鸟 (bird)

  • 我是新来的，还是个菜鸟。I'm new here, still a noob.
  • 别笑我，我打游戏是菜鸟。Don't laugh, I'm a noob at games.
  • 这个菜鸟连基础都不会。This newbie doesn't even know the basics.

Casual internet-style term. Can be teasing.

Read more →
抗kàng造zàotough / durable / resilient

抗 (resist)造 (wear)

  • 这双鞋真抗造。These shoes are really durable.
  • 他身体特别抗造。He's got a really tough constitution.
  • 这手机挺抗造的，摔了好几次。This phone's tough, dropped it many times.

Northern/casual flavor. Often used for people or things that can take punishment.

Read more →
辣là妹mèi子zispicy girl / bold girl

辣 (spicy)妹 (girl)子 (suffix)

  • 她是个湖南辣妹子。She's a feisty Hunan girl.
  • 别惹这辣妹子。Don't mess with this spicy girl.
  • 我喜欢辣妹子那种性格。I like that spicy-girl personality.

Colloquial and culturally flavored. Context-sensitive.

Read more →
小xiǎo屁pì孩háilittle brat

小 (little)屁 (butt)孩 (kid)

  • 你个小屁孩懂什么？What do you know, you little brat?
  • 一群小屁孩在楼下踢球。A bunch of brats are playing ball downstairs.
  • 别跟小屁孩一般见识。Don't stoop to a kid's level.

Casual insult for a child or immature person. Use carefully.

Read more →
家jiā伙huoguy / fellow / bloke

家 (home)伙 (fellow)

  • 这家伙真有意思。This guy is really funny.
  • 那家伙又迟到了。That dude is late again.
  • 你这家伙，跑哪儿去了？You, where'd you run off to?

Casual word for a person; tone depends on context.

Read more →
无wú辣là不bù欢huānnot spicy, not happy / can’t enjoy food without spice

无 (no)辣 (spicy)不 (not)欢 (happy)

  • 我是无辣不欢的人。I can't enjoy food without spice.
  • 四川人基本都无辣不欢。Sichuan people basically need spice in everything.
  • 她无辣不欢，顿顿都要辣椒。She needs spice every meal.

Fixed colloquial expression. Good for describing someone who loves spicy food.

Read more →
儿ér豁huōno cap / for real

儿 (son)豁 (deceive[dialect])

  • 我说的是真的，儿豁！I'm telling the truth, no cap!
  • 儿豁，我亲眼看见的。For real, I saw it with my own eyes.
  • 他真的来了，儿豁！He really came, no joke!

Sichuan/Chongqing dialect slang. Used to mean “seriously,” “for real,” or “I’m not lying.” Recognition first unless you know the context.

Read more →
牛niú逼bīawesome / badass / impressive

牛 (cow)逼 (vulgar)

  • 你太牛逼了！You're freaking awesome!
  • 这操作真牛逼。That move was badass.
  • 他英语说得贼牛逼。His English is insanely good.

Very common slang, but vulgar because the literal wording is obscene. Also written 牛B or 牛. Use carefully.

Read more →

tone-risky 5

变biàn态tàipervert / abnormal

变 (change)态 (state)

  • 你这个变态，别碰我！You pervert, don't touch me!
  • 这题难得变态。This problem is insanely hard.
  • 他笑得有点变态。His smile is kind of creepy.

Can be insulting or serious depending on context. Use carefully.

Read more →
辣là妹mèi子zispicy girl / bold girl

辣 (spicy)妹 (girl)子 (suffix)

  • 她是个湖南辣妹子。She's a feisty Hunan girl.
  • 别惹这辣妹子。Don't mess with this spicy girl.
  • 我喜欢辣妹子那种性格。I like that spicy-girl personality.

Colloquial and culturally flavored. Context-sensitive.

Read more →
你nǐ皮pí子zi是shì不bú是shì痒yǎng了le?are you asking for a beating?

你 (you)皮 (skin)子 (suffix)是 (be)不 (not)痒 (itch)了 (particle)

  • 你皮子是不是痒了？敢顶嘴！You asking for a beating? Talking back?!
  • 再闹，你皮子是不是痒了？Keep it up, you want a smack?
  • 你皮子是不是痒了？我揍你啊。You itching for it? I'll hit you.

Rude/playful threat-like phrase. Recognition first; be careful using it.

Read more →
瞅chǒu啥shá呀yawhat the hell are you looking at?

瞅 (look)啥 (what)呀 (sentence-final)

  • 瞅啥呀，没见过啊？What you looking at, never seen one?
  • 你瞅啥呀，有事吗？What you staring at, got a problem?
  • 瞅啥呀，再瞅揍你。What you looking at? Keep it up and I'll hit you.

Northern/rude confrontational phrase. Very tone-sensitive; mostly recognition first.

Read more →
牛niú逼bīawesome / badass / impressive

牛 (cow)逼 (vulgar)

  • 你太牛逼了！You're freaking awesome!
  • 这操作真牛逼。That move was badass.
  • 他英语说得贼牛逼。His English is insanely good.

Very common slang, but vulgar because the literal wording is obscene. Also written 牛B or 牛. Use carefully.

Read more →

useful-phrases 1

无wú辣là不bù欢huānnot spicy, not happy / can’t enjoy food without spice

无 (no)辣 (spicy)不 (not)欢 (happy)

  • 我是无辣不欢的人。I can't enjoy food without spice.
  • 四川人基本都无辣不欢。Sichuan people basically need spice in everything.
  • 她无辣不欢，顿顿都要辣椒。She needs spice every meal.

Fixed colloquial expression. Good for describing someone who loves spicy food.

Read more →

vulgar 1

牛niú逼bīawesome / badass / impressive

牛 (cow)逼 (vulgar)

  • 你太牛逼了！You're freaking awesome!
  • 这操作真牛逼。That move was badass.
  • 他英语说得贼牛逼。His English is insanely good.

Very common slang, but vulgar because the literal wording is obscene. Also written 牛B or 牛. Use carefully.

Read more →

Phonetic Components 476

方fāngsquare / power or involution (mathematics) / upright / honest / fair and square / direction / side / party (to a contract, dispute etc) / place / method / prescription (medicine) / just when / only or just / classifier for square things / abbr. for square or cubic meter
phonetic in: 芳坊钫鈁枋
same syllable, different tone: 放房防访仿妨纺肪舫鲂髣訪
also in 69 more chars (see HanziCraft link)
5 / 17 / 86freq #60

Note similarity to 万, which means ten
thousand.
Exceptions (different sound): 旁斿族敫於旋

羊yángsheep / CL:頭|头[tou2],隻|只[zhi1]

same syllable, different tone: 样养氧痒漾恙養
also in 90 more chars (see HanziCraft link)

Variant: ⺶，⺷

Used as a radical (sheep/livestock theme) in many chars where it's NOT the phonetic — e.g. 美, 善, 群. Watch for both roles.

Visually similar to 甲 (jiǎ) and 由 (yóu). 申 has the vertical going through both top and bottom.

Variant: 衤
As a radical the form is 衤 (left) or 衣 split (top/bottom). The phonetic role is much rarer than the semantic one.
